title: 02_PreProcssing
text: Preprocessing standardizes spatial transcriptomics data prior infection analysis. ISTtools, step ensures input data normalized, dimensionally reduced, annotated consistent format compatible downstream modules. vignette describes: Seurat-based preprocessing workflow Cell-type annotation using reference mapping

title: 02_PreProcssing
code: suppressMessages({   library(tidyverse)   library(ISTools) })

title: 02_PreProcssing
text: ISTtools provides wrapper function streamline Seurat-based preprocessing, including normalization, feature selection, dimensionality reduction, clustering.
code: Seurat_pipeline(   seurat_obj = NULL,   data_format = "stereo",   data_type = "stRNA",   resolution_index = seq(0.1, 1.3, 0.2),   runTSNE_index = FALSE,   assay_nm = NULL )

title: 02_PreProcssing
text: preprocessing pipeline performs: Data normalization scaling Identification highly variable features Principal component analysis (PCA) Graph-based clustering across multiple resolutions parameter resolution_index allows exploration clustering granularity, may influence downstream spatial interpretation.

title: 02_PreProcssing
text: facilitate biological interpretation, spatial spots can annotated using reference-based methods.
code: anno_SingleR(   seurat_obj = NULL,   ref_obj = NULL,   seurat_colnm = NULL,   ref_colnm = NULL,   species_index = NULL,   assay_nm = "Spatial",   layer_nm = "data" )

title: 02_PreProcssing

title: 03_Background_correction
text: Reliable detection infection signals spatial transcriptomics data requires explicit modeling background contamination. ISTtools, background correction performed leveraging reference samples estimate distribution pathogen-associated features remove spurious signals. step defines statistical baseline downstream analyses.

title: 03_Background_correction
code: suppressMessages({   library(tidyverse)   library(ISTools) })

title: 03_Background_correction
text: Background signals estimated using designated reference samples represent non-infected baseline conditions.
code: IST_obj_before <- IST_obj  IST_obj_after <- CorrectBackgroud(   IST_obj = IST_obj_before,   bg_samp_id = c("DPI_0_1"),   bg_features = pathogen_genes,   PosThres_prob = 0.95,   assay_id = "Spatial",   layer_id = "counts",   grp_nm = "20251201_CE_0.95",   dir_nm = "M1_CorrectBackgroud" )

title: 03_Background_correction
text: bg_samp_id: defines background reference samples bg_features: pathogen-associated features used modeling PosThres_prob: probability threshold controlling signal retention choice PosThres_prob determines balance sensitivity specificity calibrated according sequencing depth expected pathogen load.

title: 03_Background_correction
text: Background correction applied prior spatial classification corrected object preserves original structure updating signal estimates Outputs stored downstream reuse meta.data

title: 04_Infection_spot_detection
text: Following background correction, infection-positive spatial locations identified based aggregated pathogen-derived expression. ISTtools supports gene-level gene-set-based detection strategies accommodate diverse pathogen types.

title: 04_Infection_spot_detection
code: suppressMessages({   library(tidyverse)   library(ISTools) })

title: 04_Infection_spot_detection
text: improve robustness, pathogen signals first aggregated across informative features.
code: high_exp_genes <- GetTopGenes(   IST_obj,   top_n = 10,   pattern = "EmuJ-",   grp_by_samp = FALSE,   grp_by_celltype = FALSE,   assay_id = "Spatial",   layer_id = "counts" )  IST_obj <- GetGeneStat(   IST_obj = IST_obj,   features = high_exp_genes,   prefix = "top10_gene",   func = "sum" )

title: 04_Infection_spot_detection
code: IST_obj <- SpotDetect_Gene(   IST_obj,   features = high_exp_genes,   feature_colnm = grep("all_gene", colnames(IST_obj@meta.data), value = TRUE),   PosThres_prob = 0,   PosThres_count = 3,   col = COLOR_DIS_CON,   black_bg = FALSE,   pt_size = 1,   mode_SpatialPlot = "single",   grp_nm = "CE_correct_after_all_gene_white" )

title: 05_Infection_niche_detection
text: Following infection-positive spot identification, next step delineate spatially coherent infection niches. Rather treating spots independently, ISTtools reconstructs infection regions grouping neighboring positive spots continuous spatial domains. step transforms discrete detection results biologically interpretable infection niches, enabling region-level analysis.

title: 05_Infection_niche_detection
code: suppressMessages({   library(tidyverse)   library(ISTools) })

title: 05_Infection_niche_detection
text: Spatial niches identified using region-based clustering. default, ISTtools supports geometric reconstruction (e.g., convex hull) define spatial domains. procedure aggregates spatially proximal infection-positive spots contiguous regions.
code: IST_obj_detect <- NicheDetect_STS(   IST_obj = IST_obj_after,   meta_key = "M1_SpotDetect_Gene_TB_correct_after_all_gene_white",   loop_id = "PI8W_1",   method_level = "region",   method_region = "convex",   update_spots = FALSE,   k_kNNdist = 4,   pos_colnm = "Label_all_gene_nFeature(sum)",   grp_nm = "TB_DBSCAN",   dir_nm = "M2_NicheDetect_STS" )

title: 05_Infection_niche_detection
text: framework applicable viral datasets.
code: IST_obj_detect <- NicheDetect_STS(   IST_obj = IST_obj,   meta_key = "M1_SpotDetect_Gene_JEV_correct_before_all_gene_white",   method_level = "region",   method_region = "convex",   update_spots = FALSE,   pos_colnm = "Label_all_gene_nFeature(sum)",   grp_nm = "STS_JEV_microbe_region",   dir_nm = "M2_NicheDetect_STS" )

title: ISTools
text: ISTools R-based toolkit designed provide unified efficient framework spatial transcriptomics analysis infectious diseases, built upon Seurat object. spatial transcriptomics increasingly applied explore disease mechanisms, including infection, standardized analytical framework user-friendly tools tailored infectious disease research remain lacking. ISTools addresses gap defining comprehensive analytical pipeline specifically infection-related spatial transcriptomics data. toolkit encompasses four main applications: accurate detection infection sites, characterization infection microenvironment, single-sample Niche analysis, cross-sample comparative temporal analysis. integrating functions cohesive framework, ISTools enhances researchers’ ability interpret mine infection-related spatial transcriptomics data rigor ease. text: function performs following analyses: Data Preprocessing: Loads normalizes spatial transcriptomics data Spatial Region Segmentation: Uses iterative k-means-like algorithm partition tissue spatial regions Spatial Entropy Calculation: Computes adjusted entropy based cell type diversity local neighborhoods Cell Type Proportion Analysis: Calculates cell type composition region Boundary Detection: Identifies boundaries different regions Visualization: Generates multiple plots including region maps, cell type distributions, pie charts, entropy heatmaps

title: ISTools: Infectious Spatiotemporal Transcriptomics — ISTools-package
text: comprehensive toolkit quality control, analysis, exploration spatial transcriptomics data. 'ISTools' aims enable users identify spatial niches, interpret cell-cell communication contexts, integrate diverse types spatial omics measurements (e.g., Visium, Slide-seq, MERFISH). package provides unified workflow extending Seurat ecosystem, featuring: Niche Detection: Robust algorithms define spatial microenvironments based cellular composition. Spatial Aggregation: Metrics quantify cell clustering spatial organization. Contextual Interaction: Enhanced ligand-receptor analysis incorporating spatial distance constraints. Advanced Visualization: Specialized plotting functions spatial maps, niche heatmaps, interaction networks. See Yulong Qin et al. (Year) doi:10.xxxx/xxxxx methodological details niche detection,

title: ISTools: Infectious Spatiotemporal Transcriptomics — ISTools-package
text: ISTools uses following options configure behaviour: ISTools.memsafe Global option call gc() memory-intensive operations (e.g., large matrix permutations). helps clean R session memory prevents swap space usage. Defaults TRUE. Setting FALSE can speed computation high-RAM environments. \codeISTools.parallel.threads Controls default number threads parallel operations (using foreach parallel). Defaults half available physical cores. Set 1 disable parallelization. ISTools.warn.seurat.v5 Show warning input Seurat object v5 format specific v4-compatible layers missing. ISTools.check.dots functions ... parameter, controls behavior argument used. Can one "warn", "stop", "silent". Defaults "warn". ISTools.msg.nichenet Show message using cached NicheNet networks speed initialization. ISTools.msg.cellchat Show message version CellChatDB loaded.
